req.title: Break-glass access (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates break-glass access for clinicians in emergencies, with mandatory audit event emission and post-event review task creation. I derive: (1) a positive EP/STC test confirming full happy-path state transition from normal→break-glass→audited; (2) a NEG test where a non-clinician role is denied access; (3) a BVA test exercising the exact boundary of a zero-length reason string (empty justification) which sits at the minimum valid input edge; (4) a DT test covering the decision matrix where patient does not exist; (5) a STC test verifying the post-event review task is created as a downstream side-effect. Each test POSTs to /BreakGlass, inspects HTTP status, then queries the audit log endpoint to assert AuditEvent presence.

tests.name: test_break_glass_grants_access_and_emits_audit_event
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid JWT
Patient with id=PAT-001 exists in the system
actions: Assert HTTP 200 and response body contains accessGranted=true
Assert at least one AuditEvent record is returned
expected: Response JSON field accessGranted is true
At least one AuditEvent with type break-glass for PAT-001 exists
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_grants_access_and_emits_audit_event(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, existing_patient_id):
    payload = {"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": "cardiac arrest"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is True

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": existing_patient_id, "type": "break-glass"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(events) >= 1, "Expected at least one AuditEvent for break-glass access"

tests.name: test_non_clinician_break_glass_is_denied
preconditions: Caller has role:receptionist (not clinician) with valid JWT
Patient with id=PAT-001 exists in the system
actions: POST /BreakGlass with payload {"patientId": "PAT-001", "reason": "emergency"} using receptionist credentials
GET /AuditEvent?entity=PAT-001&type=break-glass and assert no new event was emitted for this attempt
expected: No AuditEvent of type break-glass created for this denied attempt
trace: R-HLT-096: access denied for unauthorized role
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_clinician_break_glass_is_denied(receptionist_auth_headers, clinician_auth_headers, base_url, existing_patient_id):
    before_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": existing_patient_id, "type": "break-glass"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    before_count = len(before_resp.json().get("entry", []))

    payload = {"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": "emergency"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=receptionist_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    after_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": existing_patient_id, "type": "break-glass"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    after_count = len(after_resp.json().get("entry", []))
    assert after_count == before_count, "Denied request must not create a break-glass AuditEvent"

tests.name: test_break_glass_with_empty_reason_is_rejected
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid JWT
Patient with id=PAT-001 exists in the system
System requires a non-empty reason string for break-glass invocation
actions: POST /BreakGlass with payload {"patientId": "PAT-001", "reason": ""} (empty string at lower boundary)
Assert response body contains a validation error referencing the reason field
expected: Response body contains field-level error for reason being empty
No AuditEvent emitted for this invalid request
trace: R-HLT-096: break-glass reason is mandatory; empty reason is at the minimum invalid boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_with_empty_reason_is_rejected(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, existing_patient_id):
    payload = {"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": ""}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422), f"Expected 400/422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("issue") or []
    reason_error = any(
        "reason" in str(e).lower() for e in errors
    )
    assert reason_error, "Validation error must reference the empty reason field"

tests.name: test_break_glass_for_nonexistent_patient_returns_error
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid JWT
Patient with id=PAT-GHOST does not exist in the system
actions: Assert response error message indicates unknown patient
expected: Response body indicates patient PAT-GHOST not found
No AuditEvent emitted for nonexistent patient
trace: R-HLT-096: precondition patient exists; decision table row where patient is absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID = "PAT-GHOST-99999"

def test_break_glass_for_nonexistent_patient_returns_error(clinician_auth_headers, base_url):
    payload = {"patientId": NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID, "reason": "trauma"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 404, f"Expected 404, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    detail = str(body).lower()
    assert "patient" in detail or "not found" in detail, "Error must reference missing patient"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID, "type": "break-glass"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(events) == 0, "No AuditEvent should exist for a nonexistent patient"

tests.name: test_break_glass_triggers_post_event_review_task
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician with valid JWT
Patient with id=PAT-002 exists in the system
System is in normal state (no prior break-glass for PAT-002 in current session)
actions: Assert HTTP 200 and accessGranted=true (state transition: normal -> break-glass)
Assert at least one Task resource exists in status draft or requested
expected: accessGranted is true in response body
At least one Task with code break-glass-review exists for PAT-002
Task status is draft or requested indicating pending post-event review
trace: R-HLT-096: break-glass use shall trigger a post-event review
R-HLT-096: error pathway review task created confirmed as side-effect
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_triggers_post_event_review_task(clinician_auth_headers, base_url):
    patient_id = "PAT-002"
    payload = {"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "respiratory failure"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    task_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Task",
        params={"focus": patient_id, "code": "break-glass-review"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    tasks = task_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(tasks) >= 1, "A post-event review Task must be created after break-glass"
    statuses = {t.get("resource", {}).get("status") for t in tasks}
    assert statuses & {"draft", "requested"}, f"Task must be in draft/requested, got {statuses}"
